behaviors.description: Exponential backoff delay doubles on each consecutive failure, capped at configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily backoff math/state progression inside retry policy logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; fake clock or direct delay computation is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration/System are disqualified because behavior text is about delay growth/cap logic itself, not cross-module I/O effects.
behaviors.test_description: Test retry-delay calculator/state object directly: feed consecutive failure counts (1..N), assert delays follow 2^n growth and never exceed max cap.

behaviors.description: Retry logic detects rate-limit responses and computes correct next wait duration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug can occur at module handoff (HTTP response classifier -> retry scheduler). Unit-only tests on parser or calculator may miss wiring mistakes. REAL-INFRA: real network not required; in-process stubbed responses can reproduce. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because behavior only needs synthetic rate-limit responses/headers, not real HTTP transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware + response interpreter + wait calculator with a stub transport returning 429/limit headers; assert retry is triggered and computed delay matches policy/header semantics.

behaviors.description: After waiting, client resends the same request and returns eventual success transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module orchestration (timer/backoff + request replay + result propagation). Unit tests on resend function alone can miss lost-request or wrong-promise wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real network required; stub transport and fake timers suffice. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because real HTTP/timeouts are not required to observe resend correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that fails with rate-limit once, then succeeds; assert second attempt payload matches original request and `call()` resolves with success value without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: When retry budget is exhausted, client throws typed `QuotaExhaustedError` instead of generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is retry loop + terminal error mapping; type can be lost when propagating across layers. REAL-INFRA: real network unnecessary; repeated stubbed rate-limit responses reproduce path. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because behavior is error-typing at exhaustion, not real transport semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries small; stub transport always returns rate-limit; run `call()` and assert rejection is instance of `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected typed fields, not raw/generic error.

behaviors.description: Entire retry chain (initial attempt, backoff, quota tracking) terminates and never loops indefinitely.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: termination depends on interaction of counters, backoff scheduler, and quota state. Unit tests of any one component can miss cyclic orchestration bugs. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with fake timers + bounded test timeout; no real OS/network needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because infinite-loop risk here is in local retry-state logic, reproducible in-process.
behaviors.test_description: With stub transport that keeps returning rate-limit, advance fake timers and assert the promise rejects after configured retry ceiling; add guard timeout/assertion to fail if loop does not terminate.

behaviors.description: Concurrent async callers in one process consume a single shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when multiple callers and shared quota manager interact (state handoff/race), not in isolated function math. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS primitive required; controlled async interleavings with stubs are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because behavior scope is in-process shared state across callers, explicitly same process.
behaviors.test_description: Create one client instance with shared quota manager; fire many concurrent `call()` invocations against stub transport; assert aggregate allowed/blocked behavior matches one global bucket rather than per-call independent quotas.

behaviors.description: Queued waiting requests are dispatched FIFO by original submission order.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs arise in queue + scheduler + release logic interaction; single-function tests can miss async reordering defects. REAL-INFRA: no real network needed; deterministic stubs and controlled timing can expose ordering failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System disqualified because queue order semantics are internal library behavior, not dependent on external OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple calls quickly while quota blocks immediate send; release capacity stepwise via stubbed quota/time controls; assert dispatch sequence matches submission order exactly.
